what_it_does: A thin argparse-based CLI that asks the dragnet/determine engine (runtime.recall_determine) a topic and prints the resulting grounded claims as JSON, truncated to 3000 characters, plus a no_fiction flag and elapsed time.
observations: Thin wrapper around an engine function; the engine is imported inside main() rather than at module top.
Inserts the repo root into sys.path so runtime can be imported.
Prints json.dumps(res, indent=2) sliced to [:3000] before the timing line.
Docstring frames it as a no-fiction, chunk-traced claim determination tool.
standouts: Output is deliberately truncated, which can hide the full determine result.
Imports the engine lazily inside main, coupling CLI startup to the engine's import cost.
Strong emphasis on 'no-fiction' / grounded claims in the docstring and final printed flag.
conventions: Uses argparse with a main() entrypoint and __main__ guard.
Uses sys.path.insert(0, REPO) for local package discovery.
Includes a usage example in the module docstring.
concerns: Truncating JSON output to 3000 characters may produce invalid or misleading output.
No error handling around engine call, JSON serialization, or missing runtime module.
sys.path mutation is a file-local import hack.
No logging or exit codes to signal success/failure.
notes: A minimal shell entrypoint that delegates all real work to runtime.recall_determine and only formats/truncates the response.
questions: What exact schema does runtime.recall_determine.determine return?
What is the 'visual-dna' asset and how does it differ from 'full'?
What does the no_fiction flag signify and who consumes it?
Why is the printed JSON capped at 3000 characters?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it is a thin CLI over the determine engine.
intention_signals: Expose the grounded dragnet determine engine as an easy-to-run command-line tool for asking natural-language questions and getting chunk-traced, non-fictional claims back.
contribution: Provides a command-line invocation path for the determine engine, accepting topic and asset parameters and surfacing the no_fiction flag and timing to the user.
summary_for_embedding: ops/dragnet_determine.py is a thin argparse CLI that calls runtime.recall_determine.determine for a given topic and prints a truncated JSON response along with a no_fiction flag and elapsed time.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a shell-accessible command-line interface to the grounded dragnet determine engine.

symbols.does: Parses CLI arguments and invokes the grounded determine engine, printing a truncated JSON result with timing and no_fiction status.
symbols.description: The main entrypoint for the dragnet determine CLI. It configures argument parsing for --topic, --asset, and --max-claims, imports runtime.recall_determine inside the function, calls determine(), then prints the first 3000 characters of the JSON output followed by the no_fiction flag and elapsed seconds.
